summary: Board authorized management to contact Industry Participant regarding exploratory discussions for a merger or acquisition
Industry Participant's CEO informed Mr. Lenhardt that Industry Participant was not for sale and raised antitrust concerns
Representatives of Longview met with certain members of the board and senior management, presenting views on the business and strategic direction of the Company
Board reviewed strategic and financial alternatives with Wachtell Lipton and a financial advisor; established ad hoc committee; authorized retention of a financial advisor
Company retained J. P. Morgan as financial advisor after interviewing several potential financial advisors
JANA Partners filed Schedule 13D disclosing 9.9% stake in PetSmart and intent to engage in discussions regarding strategic alternatives including a sale
Longview made public a letter to the board advocating for a sale of the Company and indicating willingness to roll equity into acquiring entity

counterparty_names: 27 potential participants in the sale process, including three strategic parties (not counting Industry Participant) and 24 financial participants
summary: During mid-August through end of October, J. P. Morgan was contacted by 27 potential participants in the sale process (3 strategic, 24 financial)
Industry Participant contacted J. P. Morgan indicating potential interest in re-visiting combination discussions if the Company pursued strategic alternatives
Board determined to explore strategic alternatives including a possible sale; authorized ad hoc committee to oversee sale exploration process; planned outreach to financial and strategic parties
PetSmart issued a press release announcing it determined to explore strategic alternatives to maximize stockholder value, including a possible sale of the Company
Company issued press release announcing determination to explore strategic alternatives including a possible sale
J. P. Morgan informed Industry Participant that it would not be invited to participate in the exploratory process due to board concerns, but the board would consider any unsolicited proposal
